tex:
The diagram shows a brick in the shape of a cuboid.
\newline

(a) Calculate the total surface area of the brick.
\newline

(i) (b) Calculate the total surface area of the brick.
\newline

(c) The density of the brick is 1.9 g/cm$^3$.
\newline
Work out the mass of the brick.
\newline
Give your answer in kilograms.
\newline
[Density = mass ÷ volume]

(d) 9000 bricks are needed to build a house.
\newline
200 bricks cost \$175.
\newline
Work out the cost of the bricks needed to build 5 houses.
\newline

\$ 

(e) Saskia builds a wall using 1500 bricks.
\newline
She can build at the rate of 40 bricks each hour.
\newline
She works for 9 hours each day.
\newline
Saskia starts work on 6 July and works every day until the wall is completed.
\newline
Find the date when she completes the wall.
\newline

(f) Rafa has a cylindrical tank.
\newline
The cylinder has a height of 105 cm and a diameter of 45 cm.
\newline
Calculate the capacity of the tank in litres.
\newline

content.content:

\newline

\newline
Give your answer in kilograms.
\newline
[Density = mass ÷ volume]

tex:
Bob, Chao and Mei take part in a run for charity.
\newline

(a) Their times to complete the run are in the ratio Bob : Chao : Mei = 4 : 5 : 7.
\newline

(i) (b) Find Chao's time as a percentage of Mei's time.
\newline

(c) Bob's time for the run is 55 minutes 40 seconds.
\newline
Find Mei's time for the run.
\newline
Give your answer in minutes and seconds.
\newline

(d) Chao collects \$47.50 for charity.
\newline

(i) (e) Bob collects 28\% more than Chao.
\newline
Find the amount Bob collects.
\newline

\$ 

(f) Chao collects 60\% less than Mei.
\newline
Find how much more money Mei collects than Chao.
\newline

\$ 

(g) When running, Chao has a stride length of 70 cm, correct to the nearest 5 cm.
\newline
Chao runs a distance of 11.2 km, correct to the nearest 0.1 km.
\newline
Work out the minimum number of strides that Chao could take to complete this distance.
\newline

(h) In 2015, a charity raised a total of \$1.6 million.
\newline
After 2015, this amount increased exponentially by 2.4\% each year for the next 5 years.
\newline
Work out the amount raised by the charity in 2020.
\newline

\$

content.content:
Their times to complete the run are in the ratio Bob : Chao : Mei = 4 : 5 : 7.
\newline

Find Chao's time as a percentage of Mei's time.
\newline

Bob's time for the run is 55 minutes 40 seconds.
\newline
Find Mei's time for the run.
\newline
Give your answer in minutes and seconds.
\newline

Chao collects \$47.50 for charity.
\newline

Bob collects 28\% more than Chao.
\newline
Find the amount Bob collects.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[]

Chao collects 60\% less than Mei.
\newline
Find how much more money Mei collects than Chao.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[]

When running, Chao has a stride length of 70 cm, correct to the nearest 5 cm.
\newline
Chao runs a distance of 11.2 km, correct to the nearest 0.1 km.
\newline
Work out the minimum number of strides that Chao could take to complete this distance.
\newline

In 2015, a charity raised a total of \$1.6 million.
\newline
After 2015, this amount increased exponentially by 2.4\% each year for the next 5 years.
\newline
Work out the amount raised by the charity in 2020.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][million]

stem.content:
Bob, Chao and Mei take part in a run for charity.
\newline

tex:
The cumulative frequency diagram shows information about the mass, m kg, of each of 80 boys.
\newline

(a) On the grid, draw a box-and-whisker plot to show the information in the cumulative frequency diagram.
\newline

(b) Use the cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate of

(i) (c) the 30th percentile,

(d) the number of boys with a mass greater than 75 kg.
\newline

(e) Use the cumulative frequency diagram to complete this frequency table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Mass (m kg) & Frequency \\
\hline
30 ≤ m < 40 & 8 \\
\hline
40 ≤ m < 50 & 12 \\
\hline
50 ≤ m < 60 & 14 \\
\hline
60 ≤ m < 70 & 10 \\
\hline
70 ≤ m < 80 &  \\
\hline
80 ≤ m < 90 &  \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (f) Complete the frequency table.
\newline

(g) Calculate an estimate of the mean mass of the boys.
\newline

(h) Two boys are chosen at random from those with a mass greater than 70 kg.
\newline
Find the probability that one of them has a mass greater than 80 kg and the other has a mass
of 80 kg or less.
\newline
